title: Mention of another participant; the agent's alias appears only in passing prose
predicted_basis: live failure class 2026-07-08: bare occurrences of the agent's name in prose triggered wakes even though the message was structurally addressed to someone else
failure_mode: seeing its own alias ('Vigil') anywhere in the text, the agent treats a message @-mentioning another participant as its turn and butts in
invariant: an alias in passing prose is a reference, not an address — and a structured @mention of another participant is likewise referential evidence, not proof of exclusive targeting; whose turn it is stays a semantic judgment
rationale: The @mention targets a different participant's snowflake; 'Vigil' is only credited for earlier work. Expected ground truth stays PASS — but as a MODEL-scored judgment. The deterministic fast-path no longer short-circuits foreign mentions (removed 2026-07-10: a live false PASS proved a foreign mention cannot prove the floor went elsewhere — the operator's direct correction of an agent was hard-PASSed at 1.0 with no model call). This fixture and the 2026-07-08 over-wake class it pins are the two sides the classifier must now hold together: name-in-prose must not force a wake, and mention-of-another must not force silence.
